name: adsec
description: Active Directory security assessment: enumeration, Kerberoasting, AS-REP roasting, password spraying, BloodHound collection, vuln checks, and loot extraction — all in one module
author: 0xb0rn3 | github.com/0xb0rn3

optional_dependencies.nmap: SMB/LDAP/Kerberos NSE scripts - apt install nmap
optional_dependencies.smbclient: SMB share access - apt install smbclient
optional_dependencies.netexec: AD swiss army knife (formerly crackmapexec) - pipx install netexec
optional_dependencies.kerbrute: Kerberos user enum and spraying - go install github.com/ropnop/kerbrute@latest
optional_dependencies.smbmap: SMB share content discovery - pipx install smbmap
optional_dependencies.bloodhound-python: Domain graph collection - pipx install bloodhound
optional_dependencies.ldap3: Pure-Python LDAP fallback - pip install ldap3
optional_dependencies.requests: HTTP probing for ADCS - pip install requests

help.description: Active Directory pentest framework. Covers the full attack chain from unauthenticated discovery through credential-based enumeration, Kerberoasting, AS-REP roasting, password spraying with lockout awareness, BloodHound collection, common vuln checks (Zerologon, PetitPotam, NoPac, MachineAccountQuota), and post-compromise loot. Pure-Python fallbacks via impacket/ldap3 mean it works even without external tools.

operation.description: Which AD assessment phase to run
examples: discover    - DC fingerprint + domain identification (no creds needed)
users       - User enumeration via RID brute / LDAP / Kerberos
groups      - Group enumeration including nested membership
shares      - SMB share enumeration with permissions
kerberoast  - Find SPNs and dump TGS hashes (creds required)
asreproast  - AS-REP roast users without preauth (no creds needed)
spray       - Password spray with lockout safety
vulncheck   - Zerologon, PetitPotam, NoPac, MachineAccountQuota checks
bloodhound  - Collect BloodHound JSON for graph analysis
loot        - Search shares for credentials, GPP cpassword, sensitive files
secrets     - Dump SAM/LSA/NTDS (admin or DA required)
auto        - Run all safe operations with given context
exec        - Execute a shell command on the target (creds required)
privesc_check - Audit Windows privesc vectors (unquoted paths, writable binaries, UAC, stored creds)

hash.description: NTLM hash for pass-the-hash (LM:NT or just NT)

kerberos.description: Use Kerberos authentication instead of NTLM

dc_ip.description: Domain controller IP (auto-detected from target if blank)

userlist.description: Path to file containing usernames (one per line) for spraying / asreproast

passlist.description: Path to file containing passwords for spraying

single_password.description: Single password to spray against all users (fastest)

safe_spray.description: Lockout-aware spraying — read passpol first, leave 1-2 attempts buffer

output_dir.description: Directory to save hashes, BH zips, and loot

rid_max.description: Max RID to brute when enumerating users via SAMR

exclude_users.description: Comma-separated usernames to exclude from spray (avoid lockout)

examples.description: AS-REP roast all users in a list (no creds needed)
Run every safe operation with a low-priv account

operations.discover: DC fingerprint, domain SID, OS detection, NetBIOS, anonymous SMB/LDAP, null session test, DNS records
operations.users: Enumerate domain users via LDAP (auth) / SAMR RID brute / Kerberos pre-auth probing (unauth)
operations.groups: Enumerate domain groups, members, nested membership, Domain Admins, Enterprise Admins
operations.shares: Map all SMB shares, test READ/WRITE per share, list root contents of accessible shares
operations.passpol: Pull domain password policy: lockout threshold, observation window, complexity, min length
operations.kerberoast: Discover SPNs via LDAP and request TGS for each — output hashcat-format hashes
operations.asreproast: Probe users for DONT_REQUIRE_PREAUTH flag and dump AS-REP hashes (offline crackable)
operations.spray: Lockout-aware credential spraying — checks passpol first, sprays single password across all users
operations.vulncheck: Zerologon CVE-2020-1472, PetitPotam, NoPac CVE-2021-42278/42287, MachineAccountQuota, ADCS detection
operations.bloodhound: Run bloodhound-python collection or pure-Python LDAP collection — outputs BH-compatible JSON zip
operations.loot: Search readable shares for cred files: web.config, unattend.xml, GPP groups.xml (cpassword), .kdbx, .ssh keys
operations.secrets: Dump SAM/LSA/NTDS via secretsdump — domain admin or local admin required
operations.exec: Run arbitrary command on target via WMI (impacket-wmiexec or pure-impacket native fallback)
operations.privesc_check: Drop+run PowerShell privesc auditor: unquoted services, writable binaries, AlwaysInstallElevated, UAC, token privs, stored creds, writable PATH, autoruns
operations.auto: Pipeline: discover → vulncheck → users → groups → shares → passpol → asreproast (+ kerberoast/bloodhound/privesc_check if creds) → loot

safety_features: 🔒 Lockout-aware spraying — pulls passpol first, leaves 2-attempt buffer below threshold
🔒 exclude_users supports avoiding krbtgt/admin/Guest from spray attempts
🔒 Connection pooling and rate-limiting prevent IDS triggers
🔒 Pure-Python fallbacks mean no need to install dozens of tools
🔒 All credential operations log to output_dir for audit trail

output_structure.users: Array of {sid, rid, username, full_name, description, last_logon, pwd_last_set, kerberos_preauth, spn[], admin_count, enabled, locked, disabled}
output_structure.groups: Array of {sid, name, members[], description, type}
output_structure.shares: Array of {name, type, comment, readable, writable, root_listing[]}
output_structure.passpol: {min_length, complexity, lockout_threshold, lockout_window, observation_window, max_age, min_age, history}
output_structure.kerberoast: Array of {username, spn, hash} - hashcat $krb5tgs$23$ format
output_structure.vulnerabilities: Array of {cve, name, vulnerable, evidence, severity}
output_structure.loot: Array of {share, path, type, size, content_preview, severity}

notes: ⚠️  AUTHORIZATION REQUIRED: Only run against systems you own or have explicit written permission to test
⚠️  Spraying and Kerberoasting can trigger SIEM alerts — coordinate with blue team
⚠️  AS-REP roasting only works on users with DONT_REQUIRE_PREAUTH set (rare but valuable)
⚠️  secretsdump requires DA or local admin — destructive to victim's tradecraft if misused
💡 Start with 'discover' to understand the domain before running anything authenticated
💡 Use 'vulncheck' early — Zerologon = instant DA on unpatched DCs
💡 BloodHound collection with low-priv creds reveals all attack paths to DA
💡 'auto' mode runs the full safe pipeline — fastest path from zero to assessment report
💡 Save hashes from kerberoast/asreproast and crack offline with hashcat -m 13100 / -m 18200
🔐 Hashes are written to output_dir with timestamps — never logged to stdout
help.legal_disclaimer: This tool is for authorized security testing only. Unauthorized AD enumeration, credential attacks, or domain controller exploitation may violate computer fraud laws including CFAA in the US. Always obtain explicit written authorization.

domain_info.description: Discovered domain identity and DC properties

asreproast.description: AS-REP roastable accounts and AS-REP hashes

bloodhound.description: BloodHound output zip path and statistics

exec.description: Remote command output: {returncode, output, stderr, error}
